behaviour.mcq: Select one option. Answer revealed immediately on selection.
behaviour.long_answer: Type answer, submit, mark scheme bullets appear. No grade given.

questions.stem: The anaplerotic reaction that replenishes oxaloacetate in the TCA cycle is catalysed by:
De novo fatty acid synthesis occurs in which cellular compartment?
Which reaction is the committed, rate-limiting step of fatty acid synthesis?

options.A: Condensation of acetyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA by fatty acid synthase
options.B: Formation of malonyl-CoA by acetyl-CoA carboxylase
options.D: Export of citrate from the mitochondrion

questions.stem: Which apolipoprotein is the structural protein of LDL and is synthesised exclusively in the liver?
Which of the following is INCORRECT regarding cholesterol metabolism?

options.A: HMG-CoA reductase is the rate-limiting enzyme of cholesterol synthesis
options.B: Cholesterol synthesis occurs in the cytoplasm and endoplasmic reticulum
options.C: CETP esterifies cholesterol for incorporation into lipoproteins
options.D: Mevalonate is an intermediate in cholesterol synthesis

questions.stem: Nascent chylomicrons are produced and secreted by:
Pancreatic lipase requires which protein cofactor to function in the presence of bile salts?
Which lipoprotein has the highest percentage of triglyceride content?
Which of the following is correct regarding beta-oxidation of fatty acids?

options.A: It occurs in the cytoplasm and requires NADPH
options.B: It requires carnitine to transport short-chain fatty acids into the mitochondrion
options.C: It produces FADH₂ and NADH and occurs in the mitochondrial matrix
options.D: It is the same pathway as fatty acid synthesis but run in reverse

questions.stem: The fatty acid synthase (FAS) complex contains how many essential sulfhydryl (-SH) groups?
ApoC-II activates which enzyme to enable triglyceride hydrolysis in peripheral tissues?
Which two nitrogen-containing compounds donate the two nitrogen atoms that appear in urea?
Argininosuccinate synthase catalyses the condensation of:
5-Fluorouracil (5-FU) exerts its anti-cancer effect by inhibiting:
Thioredoxin participates in the reduction of ribonucleotides via its:

options.A: Two histidine residues at the active site
options.B: Two cysteine residues that form a redox-active disulfide

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a free radical?
Which enzyme in the antioxidant system directly converts H₂O₂ to water and oxygen?
Polyubiquitin attachment to a protein primarily targets it for:
Which antioxidant enzyme converts superoxide (O₂•⁻) to hydrogen peroxide?
Define phosphofructokinase-1 (PFK-1) and its role as the key regulatory enzyme of glycolysis.
mark_scheme: Catalyses F6P + ATP → F1,6-BP; first committed irreversible step of glycolysis
Rate-limiting / pacemaker enzyme of the pathway

questions.stem: List and explain ALL allosteric activators of PFK-1, including the role of fructose-2,6-bisphosphate (F-2,6-BP) and how it is regulated by PFK-2.
mark_scheme: Activators: AMP, ADP, Pi, fructose-2,6-bisphosphate (F-2,6-BP)
F-2,6-BP is the most potent activator
Produced by the kinase domain of the bifunctional enzyme PFK-2/FBPase-2
Insulin (fed state) → PFK-2 kinase active → high F-2,6-BP → PFK-1 strongly activated
Glucagon (fasting) → PKA phosphorylates PFK-2 → phosphatase domain active → F-2,6-BP degraded → PFK-1 activity falls

questions.stem: List and explain ALL allosteric inhibitors of PFK-1.
mark_scheme: ATP at high concentration (allosteric site distinct from substrate site; signals energy surplus)
Citrate (signals TCA saturation / acetyl-CoA surplus)
H⁺ / low pH (prevents glycolysis exacerbating lactic acidosis)

questions.stem: Explain why long-chain fatty acids cannot freely cross the inner mitochondrial membrane.
mark_scheme: Inner mitochondrial membrane is impermeable to CoA and fatty acyl-CoA
Long-chain fatty acids (>C12) require the carnitine shuttle
Short/medium-chain fatty acids (<C12) cross freely without carnitine

questions.stem: Compare beta-oxidation and fatty acid synthesis in terms of: (i) cellular location, (ii) key cofactors used, (iii) carbon-carrier molecule (CoA vs ACP).
mark_scheme: Location: beta-oxidation in the mitochondrial matrix; synthesis in the cytoplasm
Cofactors: beta-oxidation uses FAD and NAD⁺; synthesis requires NADPH, ATP, and biotin
Carbon carrier: CoA (beta-oxidation) vs ACP — acyl carrier protein (synthesis)

questions.stem: Define a free radical and distinguish it from a non-radical reactive oxygen species, using H₂O₂ and OH• as examples.
mark_scheme: Free radical: any species with ≥1 unpaired electron in its outer orbital; highly reactive, initiates chain reactions
OH• IS a radical — one unpaired electron; most reactive biological radical
H₂O₂ is NOT a radical — all electrons paired; it is a reactive oxygen species but not a free radical

questions.stem: Describe three antioxidant enzyme systems (superoxide dismutase, catalase, glutathione peroxidase), including their reactions and subcellular locations.
mark_scheme: SOD: 2O₂•⁻ + 2H⁺ → H₂O₂ + O₂; Cu/Zn-SOD in cytoplasm, Mn-SOD in mitochondria
Catalase: 2H₂O₂ → 2H₂O + O₂; located in peroxisomes
Glutathione peroxidase (GPx): H₂O₂ + 2GSH → 2H₂O + GSSG; GSSG recycled to GSH by glutathione reductase using NADPH

questions.stem: Explain how G6PD connects the pentose phosphate pathway to antioxidant defence via glutathione.
mark_scheme: G6PD catalyses the first step of the PPP → produces NADPH
NADPH powers glutathione reductase → regenerates GSH from GSSG
Without G6PD: insufficient NADPH → GSH depleted → ROS accumulate → oxidative damage
RBCs especially vulnerable — no mitochondria, so PPP is their ONLY source of NADPH

questions.stem: Give one DESIRABLE and one HARMFUL role of free radicals in human physiology, with specific examples.
mark_scheme: DESIRABLE: respiratory burst — NADPH oxidase in neutrophils/macrophages generates O₂•⁻ → kills pathogens (innate immunity)
HARMFUL: lipid peroxidation of membrane PUFAs → self-propagating chain reaction → membrane disruption; OR DNA strand breaks → mutations / carcinogenesis

questions.stem: Which of the following statements about succinate dehydrogenase is CORRECT?

options.A: It is soluble in the mitochondrial matrix
options.B: It produces NADH during oxidation of succinate to fumarate
options.C: It is the only TCA enzyme embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane
options.D: It contains FMN as its prosthetic group

questions.stem: During glycolysis, NADH is produced at which step?
Which of the following is NOT true about glycogen?

options.A: It is a branched polymer of glucose monomers
options.B: Muscle glycogen can be exported as free glucose into the bloodstream
options.C: Its breakdown is stimulated by glucagon and epinephrine
options.D: It contains alpha-1,4 glycosidic bonds in its linear segments

questions.stem: Which of the following correctly describes galactose metabolism?

options.A: Galactose is phosphorylated by hexokinase to galactose-6-phosphate
options.B: The first step is phosphorylation to galactose-1-phosphate by galactokinase
options.C: Galactose enters glycolysis directly as glucose-6-phosphate
options.D: UDP-galactose is not involved in galactose catabolism

questions.stem: During the oxidative phase of the pentose phosphate pathway, each glucose-6-phosphate molecule yields:
Glycogen synthase is regulated such that:

options.A: It is activated by phosphorylation by PKA and GSK-3
options.B: It is inactivated by phosphorylation and activated by dephosphorylation
options.C: It is activated by glucagon through cAMP signalling
options.D: It is activated by AMP allosterically

questions.stem: Which of the following statements about PFK-1 regulation is CORRECT?

options.A: PFK-1 is activated by high concentrations of ATP
options.B: Pyruvate kinase is allosterically inhibited by alanine
options.C: Hexokinase is allosterically activated by glucose-6-phosphate

questions.stem: The malate-aspartate shuttle:

options.A: Transfers cytoplasmic NADH equivalents into the mitochondria, yielding the same ATP as mitochondrial NADH
options.B: Transfers FADH₂ reducing equivalents from cytoplasm to mitochondria
options.C: Is used in skeletal muscle as the primary NADH transfer system
options.D: Uses glycerol-3-phosphate as its key intermediate

questions.stem: Which lipoprotein is the primary carrier of triglycerides from the LIVER to peripheral tissues?
In lipoprotein metabolism, the enzyme responsible for esterifying cholesterol WITHIN HDL particles is:
Which of the following correctly describes chylomicron maturation?

options.A: Chylomicrons are assembled in the liver and secreted into the portal circulation
options.B: ApoB-100 is the structural apolipoprotein of nascent chylomicrons
options.C: Nascent chylomicrons acquire ApoC-II and ApoE from HDL in the circulation
options.D: Chylomicron remnants are cleared by the lungs

questions.stem: Ketone bodies are produced in the liver when:

options.A: Glycogen stores are full and glucose supply is high
options.B: Acetyl-CoA from beta-oxidation exceeds the capacity of the TCA cycle due to low oxaloacetate
options.C: NADPH levels are elevated in the cytoplasm
options.D: Malonyl-CoA concentrations are elevated, stimulating beta-oxidation

questions.stem: Complete beta-oxidation of palmitoyl-CoA (16-carbon saturated fatty acid) produces:
Which amino acid serves as the primary nitrogen carrier from peripheral tissues to the liver?
In pyrimidine biosynthesis, the rate-limiting first committed step in the cytoplasm is catalysed by:
Vitamin B₁₂ absorption requires all of the following EXCEPT:

options.A: R-proteins (haptocorrins) in saliva for initial binding
options.B: Intrinsic factor secreted by gastric parietal cells
options.C: Pancreatic proteases to release B₁₂ from R-proteins in the duodenum
options.D: Bile salts for micelle formation in the ileum

questions.stem: Which of the following amino acids contributes the most atoms directly to the pyrimidine ring?

options.B: Aspartate (contributes N1, C4, C5, C6 — the bulk of the ring)
options.C: Glycine (contributes C and N directly to the ring)

questions.stem: The respiratory burst in phagocytes uses NADPH oxidase to produce which primary reactive species for pathogen killing?
Briefly explain why the TCA cycle requires aerobic conditions even though it does not directly consume oxygen.
mark_scheme: The TCA cycle does not directly consume O₂
It uses NAD⁺ and FAD as electron acceptors → produces NADH and FADH₂
NADH and FADH₂ must be continuously re-oxidised by the electron transport chain (ETC)
The ETC requires O₂ as the terminal electron acceptor (O₂ → H₂O at Complex IV)
Without O₂: ETC halts → NAD⁺/FAD fully reduced → TCA cycle arrests

questions.stem: Explain how the fumarate produced in the urea cycle is recycled back to aspartate, and why this represents a link between the urea cycle and the TCA cycle.
mark_scheme: Fumarate is produced by argininosuccinate lyase (step 4, cytoplasm)
Fumarate → fumarase → malate
Malate → malate dehydrogenase → oxaloacetate (OAA) + NADH
OAA + glutamate → aspartate aminotransferase (AST) → aspartate + α-ketoglutarate
Aspartate re-enters the urea cycle at argininosuccinate synthase (step 3), providing the second nitrogen atom
This is the urea cycle–TCA cycle ‘bicycle’: TCA intermediates supply the aspartate nitrogen carrier
